hypothesis: Fine-tuning a smaller, domain-specialized language model on the MatPROV dataset's PROV-JSONLD synthesis graphs will achieve higher structural-level F1-scores than the best-performing general-purpose LLM (o4-mini with one-shot prompting) when evaluated on synthesis procedures from material classes underrepresented in the training corpus (e.g., MOFs or gold nanorods), because explicit exposure to the graph-theoretic provenance schema during fine-tuning will generalize the structural reasoning required for branching and converging synthesis topologies more robustly than in-context prompting alone.
